news.text: Ineos Automotive: Startup backed by a knighted billionaire and soccer mogul wants to rekindle the rugged SUV market
Wall Street futures drop after US-Iran peace talks fail - Reuters
Gold falls as renewed US-Iran tensions dampen rate cut hopes - Reuters
UK will not back blockade of Strait of Hormuz, PM Starmer says - Reuters
China urges restraint over US blockade of Strait of Hormuz, backs talks - Reuters
Pope says he will continue to speak out against war after Trump attack - Reuters

brief:
◆ Market Overview

Mixed pre-market action with NASDAQ (+0.14%) outperforming while DOW (-0.55%) leads the decline. VIX spike to 30.77 (+1.25%) signals growing anxiety as US-Iran peace talks collapse and geopolitical tensions escalate around the Strait of Hormuz.

◆ What to Watch

  • Geopolitical Developments: Failed US-Iran talks dominating sentiment with potential Strait of Hormuz blockade threatening global supply chains
  • Rate Cut Expectations: Growing tensions dampening Fed dovish bets as gold retreats despite safe-haven demand
  • China Export Impact: AI-driven boom under threat as Iran conflict disrupts trade routes
  • Light Earnings Day: Minor names reporting with no major catalyst from corporate results

◆ Sector Rotation

Tech (+0.39%) and Materials (+0.56%) leading while defensive plays get hammered - Health (-1.35%) and Staples (-1.29%) worst performers. Risk-on/risk-off tug of war with growth beating value as traders bet on AI resilience despite macro headwinds.
⚡ Key Driver
Strait of Hormuz tensions could spike oil prices and reignite inflation fears, killing rate cut hopes and pressuring equities
⚠ Bear Case
VIX above 30 with failed peace talks suggests more downside ahead. China export warnings signal global growth slowdown
✦ Bull Case
Tech resilience at +0.39% shows AI narrative intact. Light economic calendar means focus stays on oversold bounces

◆ Levels That Matter

  • SPY: Key support at $675, resistance at $685. Below $670 triggers deeper selling
  • QQQ: Holding $610 crucial for tech leadership. Break above $615 targets $620
  • VIX: Above 31 signals panic mode. Below 28 calms nerves
  • XLE: Energy bounce likely if oil spikes on Hormuz fears. Watch $95 crude level

Not a prediction. A preparation. Make your plan before the bell.
